properties.string_value: Calibrate 2027 mortality assumptions for term product
Implement premium floor validation in policy API
Publish reserve impact note for release 2026.09
Draft UAT scenarios for underwriting rules engine
Policy API business rules specification v2.4
Age at issue must be between 18 and 75 for product ALI-TermSecure
Preferred smoker class prohibited when cotinine score exceeds threshold
Monthly premium cannot be less than 12.50 EUR
Slack summary to engineering on mortality calibration results

## Actuary workspace overview

The test user is a life insurance actuary supporting the policy administration platform at Aurora Life Insurance. Current focus includes mortality assumptions, underwriting constraints, reserves, and release readiness.

### Team responsibilities

The Actuarial Modeling Team owns experience studies and table updates. The Policy Administration Platform Squad owns API validations and deployment workflows.

### Colleagues

Maya Patel leads product pricing. Ethan Ross leads platform engineering. Priya Nandakumar is QA lead for policy workflows. Jonas Keller is product manager for life products.

## Task backlog summary

Open tasks cover assumption calibration, API premium floor checks, reserve communication, and UAT scenario writing.

### Mortality calibration task details

Calibrate 2027 mortality assumptions using recent claims and lapse-adjusted exposure windows. Compare against standard table and produce deltas by issue age band.

#### Calibration quality checks

Include credibility weighting, anti-selection flags, and seasonality sensitivity. Document model fit statistics and caveats for regulators.

### Premium floor task details

Implement premium floor validation in policy API so monthly premium values below 12.50 EUR are rejected with a clear error code and audit entry.

### Reserve impact note task details

Summarize the reserve change effect for ALI-TermSecure and ALI-WholeLife Plus under revised mortality and smoker classification assumptions.

### UAT scenarios task details

Create scenarios for issue age boundary checks, smoker class decisioning, premium floor behavior, and quote override approval path.

## Business rules reference

Age rule, smoker eligibility rule, and premium floor rule are enforced by the underwriting decision engine and policy quote service.

### Product context

ALI-TermSecure targets level term protection with digital onboarding; ALI-WholeLife Plus includes cash-value accumulation and tighter underwriting controls.

### Component architecture

Policy quote service calls underwriting decision engine for risk class and then reserve projection pipeline for pricing guardrails.

#### Integration contract notes

The policy API validates payload shape first, then evaluates business rules, then returns a deterministic pricing decision id for auditability.

## Release 2026.09 plan

Release includes premium floor enforcement, updated smoker class thresholds, and dashboards for reserve adequacy monitoring.

### Release risk log

Key risks are stale mortality assumptions, mismatch between rules engine and API validation, and incomplete UAT for boundary ages.

### CAB review prep

Prepare one-page justification of assumption shifts, expected reserve movement, and policyholder communication impact.

### Message draft to engineering

Share mortality calibration summary, implementation dependency list, and target merge window before freeze date.

## Reporting artifact

Q3 Life Portfolio Assumption Review documents assumptions, sensitivities, and decision outcomes used for pricing and reserves.

### Reserve adequacy concept notes

Reserve adequacy monitoring tracks expected cash outflows, claim incidence drift, and premium sufficiency under adverse scenarios.

### Governance concept notes

Mortality table governance requires peer review, versioned approval, and change board signoff before production use.

### Rule-to-product mapping

Issue age and premium floor rules apply to both products, while smoker class thresholds differ by product line and channel.

### Office location note

Aurora Life HQ is in Frankfurt and hosts release war-room activities during high-risk cutovers.

### Cross-functional collaboration

Actuary collaborates with product, platform engineering, and QA to align assumptions, code behavior, and release evidence.

### Knowledge continuity

Assistant links help preserve context across actuarial analyses, sprint tasks, and release documentation.
